questions: Where exactly should the first status message appear? The plan places it inside `_start_admin_payment_flow` (right when the test payment is queued), but it could instead appear at `_gate_existing_intent`/`_gate_fresh_intent_atomic` (earlier — at admin approval) or at the moment the admin confirms the DM. Which lifecycle point is the intended anchor?

Is there a preferred wording for the in-place status progression (e.g., 'Verifying wallet…', 'Test confirmed, awaiting reply', 'Payout sending…', 'Payment sent.')? I will pick concise defaults if not.

Should the final 'Payment sent' message be a true Discord reply (threaded under the status message with `message.reply(...)`), or a normal `channel.send` that tags the recipient? The brief says 'reply' — I've assumed Discord's reply feature.

For the failure / manual-review branches, do you want the status message edited to a terminal failure line in-channel, or should failures stay silent in-channel and surface only in the admin DM? I've assumed edit-in-place.

Is it acceptable to re-use `receipt_prompt_message_id` to point at the edited status message (so the stale-receipt sweeper keeps working), or would you rather sweep logic read `status_message_id` directly?

success_criteria.criterion: `sql/admin_payment_intents.sql` gains an idempotent `add column if not exists status_message_id bigint;` block, matching the existing migration style.
`db_handler.update_admin_payment_intent` retries once with `status_message_id` removed when Supabase reports the column is unknown, and does not swallow unrelated errors.
`PaymentWorkerCog._handle_terminal_payment` does not call `_notify_payment_result` when `producer == 'admin_chat'`, but still calls it for every other producer.

A full admin_chat payout produces exactly one channel `send` for the status message (subsequently edited in place) plus one final reply tagging the recipient — no separate 'queued test payment', 'confirmation received', or multi-line 'Payment sent.' card.

The final 'Payment sent' message is a Discord reply to the status message, mentions the recipient with `<@id>`, and is sent with `suppress_embeds=True` so the Solana Explorer link does not render a link card.
`status_message_id` is persisted on `admin_payment_intents` the first time the status message is sent and is reused across later edits within the same payout.
Admin DM notifications (`_notify_intent_admin` test/final branches) remain unchanged in both content and trigger points.

`tests/test_admin_payments.py` includes new coverage for: generic-card suppression (positive and negative producer), column-absent fallback in `update_admin_payment_intent`, single-status-message reuse across the full payout lifecycle, and the final reply's recipient mention + suppress_embeds flag.
No new fallback/defensive wrappers beyond the one narrow `status_message_id` retry — no generic 'ignore unknown column' helper.
The admin_chat message helper stays small (~40 lines) and is the single place that creates/edits the status message.
Status wording is concise (single short line per transition), not multi-bullet blocks.
Manual end-to-end smoke on staging confirms one edited message + one tagged reply with no Solana embed card.

assumptions: The first status message is created inside `_start_admin_payment_flow` at the moment the test payment is queued. Earlier lifecycle points (admin approval DM, fresh-intent atomic gate) are out of scope unless the user picks another anchor.

`channel.send(..., suppress_embeds=True)` and `message.edit(..., suppress=True)` are supported by the current discord.py version in use — the codebase already relies on the same flag in `src/features/curating/curator.py:189-193`.
The status message lives in the same channel as the intent (`intent['channel_id']`). Cross-channel editing is not needed.
The final 'Payment sent' message is a Discord reply (`message.reply(...)`) to the status message, not a standalone `channel.send`.
Failure / manual-review branches edit the status message to a short terminal failure line rather than sending additional channel messages.
`receipt_prompt_message_id` is repointed to the edited status message id so the stale-receipt sweeper (`_sweep_stale_test_receipts`) keeps functioning without schema churn.
Older deployments that have not re-applied `sql/admin_payment_intents.sql` are transient — the runtime tolerance is a bridge, not a permanent feature flag. No rollback path beyond reverting the code.
No other producers currently rely on the generic `_notify_payment_result` card being suppressed. Only `admin_chat` is silenced.

Exception matching for the column-absent retry uses a simple substring check on the exception text (`'status_message_id' in str(exc)`); this is sufficient because Supabase/PostgREST error messages include the column name. Introducing Supabase-specific error classes is out of scope.
